User_Story: As an AI application developer, I want to register and ping my agent endpoint so that I can validate connectivity before running evaluations.
Business_Value: Reduces failed runs caused by invalid endpoints.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given a valid base URL, when I click Test Connection, then the platform returns success within timeout and shows a healthy state.
Test_Case: POST /api/agent/ping with valid URL returns success=true; invalid URL returns actionable error.

User_Story: As an AI application developer, I want a playground to send prompts to my agent so that I can inspect raw behavior before red-team evaluation.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given a base URL and prompt, when I send query, then the UI displays answer, metadata, and context snippets.
Test_Case: Use /sandbox and POST /api/agent/query; verify response cards render.

User_Story: As an AI safety engineer, I want to run a scenario bundle against an agent so that I can detect safety and reliability violations.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given configured provider/settings/scenarios, when evaluation starts, then status changes queued->running->completed/failed and results are persisted.
Test_Case: Create run from /dashboard/evaluations/new and verify evaluation detail record + status transitions.

User_Story: As an AI safety engineer, I want predicate-based scoring so that unsafe outputs are flagged with severity.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given harmful instructions/jailbreak execution, then status is FAIL_CRITICAL; given safe refusal, then status is PASS.
Test_Case: Run pytest tests/test_scoring_realtime_detection.py and validate expected statuses.

User_Story: As an AI safety engineer, I want JSON/Markdown reports so that I can provide audit evidence.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given a completed evaluation, then report JSON and Markdown are downloadable and structurally valid.

User_Story: As a RAG engineer, I want a health endpoint on my target agent so that platform connectivity checks are deterministic.
Acceptance_Criteria: Given the agent is initialized, GET /health returns 200 with status and documents_loaded fields.

User_Story: As a RAG engineer, I want a stable /agent contract so that any evaluator can call my service consistently.
Acceptance_Criteria: POST /agent accepts query and returns answer plus optional context_snippets and metadata.
Test_Case: curl POST /agent with JSON body and validate response schema.

User_Story: As a user, I want evaluation pages to auto-refresh while running so that I do not need manual navigation.
Business_Value: Improves usability and avoids confusion in demos.
Acceptance_Criteria: While status in queued/pending/running, page refreshes periodically; refresh stops on terminal state.
Test_Case: Start run and stay on details page until completion without leaving page.

User_Story: As a user, I want summary metrics to match report details so that score interpretation is trustworthy.
Acceptance_Criteria: UI summary counts and safety score match normalized report JSON payload.
Test_Case: Compare UI summary with downloaded report JSON for same evaluation.

User_Story: As a reviewer, I want screenshot + API proof + repo link for each sprint so that implementation claims are verifiable.
Acceptance_Criteria: Sprint folder contains screenshots, API evidence, GitHub links, and demo flow notes.
